creator: asami@FreeBSD.org
text:
I don't know if these changes should be made (leave that to the
maintainers...), but:

 * lib/libpico.so.1.3 -> libpico.so.4.1 (would 4.0.1 be valid?)

If the new one is incompatible with the old version, then the new one
should be called "libpico.so.2.0" or "libpico401.so.1.0".  (Probably
the latter, so pine3 can still be built on a system with pine4
installed.)

In no case should the shared library version number linked with the
software's release number.  (Just think what will happen if pine-4.0.2
comes out with an incompatible library.)

Satoshi
